verdict.description: approved only when no blocker or major concerns remain; minor concerns are acceptable. Otherwise changes_requested.

summary.description: One-line overall assessment of the plan in its current state

concerns.description: Every outstanding concern, grounded in a specific file or plan section. Raise everything you find now — do not hold minor items back for a later round.

severity.description: blocker = the plan cannot be executed as written, or following it produces broken / non-functional code. major = following the plan yields incorrect code or misses a goal the plan itself states. minor = clarity, rigor, or convention refinement that the plan works without (e.g. test-assertion granularity, wording, naming, a tighter type annotation in a final code block). When unsure between major and minor, it is minor.

kind.description: add = the plan is missing something it needs. cut = the plan is over-specified, redundant, or bloated and should be shortened. change = existing content should be revised. Actively look for 'cut' concerns; a shorter plan that still conveys intent is better.

location.description: The file path or plan section the concern applies to

suggestion.description: A concrete way to resolve it. For coverage gaps and process risks, phrase as a recommendation — do not mandate a required gate; that is the author's call.
